payload.text: Three days of research since the last digest (2026-07-19), covering 13 more retired strategies plus one gate-governance episode and a first market expansion. On the ms_shift line: the fold-regime follow-up falsified the '2022 whipsaw' theory for ms-shift-spy-v1 (edge looks like a few post-shock continuation folds, not regime-conditional), and a new concept page (stop-loss-rearm-coupling) documented why enforcing the manifest's stop-loss made both ms-shift variants worse until an 'Option C' re-arm fix passed OOS validation on both -- neither still clears the gate (Sharpe ~0.63-0.76 vs 1.0 minimum). Nine more single-hypothesis strategies were proposed and retired in sequence: mean-reversion-spy-qqq and turn-of-month-spy-qqq came back as clean nulls; ms-shift-spy-vol-regime and three tsmom gates (vol-acceleration, vol-acceleration-hysteresis, vol-targeting) and a fourth cross-sectional breadth gate all made tsmom-spy-qqq's baseline worse, not better; 52wk-high-spy-qqq landed close to but below tsmom's near-miss with high signal overlap. The universe was widened to 16 symbols enabling two cross-sectional strategies (sector-rotation, dual-momentum-equity-bond-gold), both underperforming the absolute-momentum baseline. The best result of the whole campaign came from combining rather than gating: tsmom-ms-shift-blend (50/50 blend of tsmom-spy-qqq and ms-shift-spy-high-displacement, correlation 0.552) hit Sharpe 0.884 / Sortino 1.297, clearing Sortino outright and missing Sharpe by only 0.116 -- still retired per the standing gate. A postmortem on universe diversity found only 2 of 16 pinned symbols (TLT, GLD) are genuine non-equity diversifiers (71.7% of variance is one PC1 factor). Governance note: the 1.0/1.2 backtest-to-paper gate (traced to a single pre-data 2026-07-19 commit) was flagged for human review twice -- an initial log entry recorded a decision to loosen it for tsmom-ms-shift-blend, which was then corrected: the human in fact declined, specifically because that same 1.0 bar is written into the blend's own pre-registered kill criterion, and undermining it now to rescue a near-miss would break the discipline that retired everything else cleanly. contracts/promotion_thresholds.toml remains untouched at 1.0/1.2. Separately, crypto was enabled as a v1 market (schema + per-market annualization for 365-day trading), a BTC/USD-ETH/USD snapshot was pinned, and tsmom-btc-eth (the same signal, calendar-day-adjusted) was tested as the vault's first non-equity strategy -- Sharpe 0.219, a clear miss that also falsifies the 'a different asset class alone unlocks a better result' hypothesis; no second crypto strategy was attempted since the miss wasn't ambiguous. Open question for the next research pass: with 14/14 real strategies now retired and both the richest equity blend (0.884) and the first crypto test closed out, is the next lever a wider search for a second uncorrelated leg to add to the tsmom/ms-shift blend, a genuinely larger (dozens-to-hundreds-name) cross-sectional universe, or a different mechanism class tried on crypto rather than a different asset class on the same mechanism?
